frontier: Smallest N that is a sum of 3 prime cubes in exactly 6 ways (A385316 a(6)).
action:
Milestone: bounded_exact_search; contract=sha256:5c3f895292d59b11fc8f37a0f70c223f24ee5aa5a6d73b5948bfd5c1e9c4c69b
Wrote and executed a second independent implementation (bounded_search_v2.py) of the O(|P|^2) bounded exact-search verifier for sums of 3 prime cubes ≤ C. Verified all five OEIS known terms at C=2e10 (a(1)=24, a(2)=185527, a(3)=8627527, a(4)=999979163, a(5)=10588881419 all PASS). Ran certified lower-bound sweeps to C=1e11 (627 primes, 31.5M distinct sums, 16.2s, a(6) not found → a(6) > 1e11).
verified:
Verifier domain: P = {p prime : p^3 ≤ C}, enumerate nondecreasing triples (p≤q≤r) with p³+q³+r³ ≤ C, count exact multiplicities of each sum, certify a(n) by mapping smallest N per count n.
Verifier predicate: for claimed N = a(6), sum_counts[N] == 6 AND for all M < N, sum_counts[M] != 6.
Artifact: bounded_search_v2.py
Known-term verification at C=2e10: a(1)..a(5) all reproduce exactly (5/5 PASS).
Lower-bound at C=1e11: 627 primes, 31,474,082 distinct sums enumerated in 16.2s, a(6) not found → certified a(6) > 1e11.
Independent implementation B reproduces implementation A's correctness claim on known terms while completing within budget.

result: Implementation B independently confirmed: a(6) > 1e11. The bounded search kernel is verified correct (5/5 known terms), efficient (16.2s for C=1e11 with 627 primes), and reproducible. The a(6) value remains unfound at this cutoff. The accepted continuation's claim of a(6) > 1e10 is now superseded by this independent verification to a(6) > 1e11.
